text: organic evolution the principle set forth by Darwin that every plant or animal has evolved, or changed, over a long period of time from earlier, simpler forms of life to more complex forms (p. 608) orthodoxy traditional beliefs, especially in religion (p. 469) ostracism in ancient Athens, the process for temporarily banning ambitious politicians from the city by popu- lar vote (p. 123) ozone layer a thin layer of gas in the upper atmosphere that shields Earth from the sun's ultraviolet rays (p.970)
